title: Daily Briefing — May 11
headline: Tech takes the lead as laggards stay sick—don't chase, follow the flows.
body: Right, so the broad tape is bullish with SPY at $737.62 approaching the 20 MA ($713.06) but the big story is tech—XLK is leading hard (RS=116.3, Mom=107.8) while everything else is lagging or outright sick. VIX is ticking higher above the 20 MA, so volatility is expanding but not screaming panic just yet. Fear & Greed's at 66.9, stubbornly greedy with no sign of movement.

Sector rotation is textbook: XLK and QQQ (+2.3%) are powering higher, with XLK now 10% above its SMA20. Meanwhile, Energy (XLE $55.70), Financials (XLF $51.24), and Utilities (XLU $44.72) are all lagging—the canaries aren't singing, they're coughing, and XLF is flirting with losing its 20 MA. Breadth is narrow, leadership is concentrated in large cap tech (AAPL, NVDA, DELL, MU), so watch out for the same old 2000 playbook.

Contrarian flags: When everyone's piling into tech with lagging breadth and Greed near 70, patience is warranted. Financials are on edge, volatility is firming, and we’re approaching sentiment and technical warning levels—but no hard rule triggers today. If it's in the press, it's in the price—don't chase these moves, abundance mindset.
callout_body:
SPY resistance: $737.62
SPY 20 MA: $713.06 (watch for mean reversion)
XLF 20 MA: $51.89 (critical for the canary)
XLK 20 MA: $158.77 (tech leadership)
VIX: 18.26 (watch for >20 acceleration)
Fear & Greed: 66.9 (high, but not extreme)
QQQ $711.23 (approaching trigger zone)
XLE $55.70 (lagging, risk high)
action_items: Monitor Financials (XLF $51.24) for support—daily close below 20 MA is a risk trigger per rule R010.
Avoid chasing Tech momentum—wait for a retest of 20 MA zones or a volatility spike for entry, per abundance mindset.
closing_quote: Less trades = more wins. If you missed the move, don’t break something that’s still simple and good.
